summary: Gruber criticizes Leopard's new Dock design as the one clearly bad cosmetic change among many improvements. He highlights the visual inconsistencies others have noted — conflicting perspectives and light sources — and how the design looks especially absurd when positioned on the side of the screen. He endorses Paul Kafasis's suggestion that Apple use a different, simpler Dock appearance for side positioning. As a Dock-on-the-side user himself, Gruber argues that vertical real estate is too precious for a bottom Dock, and that side users deserve a serious, non-silly design.
key_points: Leopard's new reflective 'shelf' Dock introduces visual inconsistencies with conflicting perspectives and light sources that violate Apple's own HIG
The Dock design looks especially absurd when positioned on the left or right side of the screen rather than the bottom
Kafasis's suggestion of a different Dock appearance for side positioning would let Apple keep its flashy bottom Dock for marketing while giving serious users a clean alternative
Vertical screen real estate is more valuable than horizontal on modern displays, making bottom Dock placement impractical
money_quotes: One, though, is clearly for the worse, so bad that one wonders how anyone at Apple can think it's a good idea: the Dock.
Vertical screen real estate is more precious than horizontal on recent-vintage displays.
The bottom has always struck me as a silly place to keep your Dock, so, it seems fair for Apple to give the Dock-on-the-bottom a silly look-and-feel.
But the Dock-on-the-side? That's a serious Dock. And it deserves a serious, non-silly, look-and-feel.
It's a brilliant out for Apple: whoever it is who's in love with the faux-3D perspective, reflective surface, and conflicting light sources can have them.
But just move your Dock over to the side, and the goofiness goes away.
key_insight: Apple should use the simple, clean Dock appearance for side positioning while keeping the flashy shelf Dock at the bottom, giving serious users an escape from gratuitous visual flair.
